primary_objective: Eliminate Day-1 abandonment and establish persistent AI coaching continuity to unlock retention and compounding user value before scaling acquisition.
critical_constraints: 74–77% Day-1 abandonment means the product has no retention foundation to build on — growth is currently a leaking bucket
AI infrastructure hard-capped at $200/month — every agent interaction must be cost-architected, not just cost-aware
Solo founder carrying product, engineering, design, and BD simultaneously — execution bandwidth is the binding constraint
No persistent agent memory — AI coaching is stateless, making personalization claims structurally false at the session boundary
No behavioral economics layer — engagement loops are absent, leaving habit formation entirely to user willpower
success_definition: Day-1 abandonment reduced from 75%+ to below 40% within one redesigned onboarding cycle
Users reach a defined 'aha moment' (first personalized workout generated and completed) within 5 minutes of signup
Persistent memory layer operational — AI agent recalls user history, preferences, and progress across sessions
AI infrastructure cost remains at or below $200/month at up to 1,000 active users via model tiering and prompt optimization
At least one behavioral retention mechanic (commitment device, streak, or variable reward) measurably improves Day-7 return rate
Solo founder can ship, monitor, and iterate without adding headcount or DevOps overhead

value_pools.description: Overwhelmed by choice — needs a frictionless, guided first workout that feels personal and achievable within minutes of signup
Needs an AI coach that remembers their history and evolves programming intelligently — stateless agents destroy this value entirely
Values a system that adapts to schedule constraints and delivers a ready-to-execute plan without configuration overhead each session
Secondary segment seeking wearable integration and recovery-aware programming — high LTV if platform can ingest and act on biometric signals

core_questions: What is the exact drop-off point in the current onboarding funnel and what is the minimum viable 'aha moment' that must be delivered before that point?
What is the lowest-cost persistent memory architecture (vector store, summarization layer, or structured profile) that keeps AI continuity within the $200/month ceiling?
Which single behavioral economics mechanic — commitment device, loss aversion trigger, or variable reward — has the highest expected impact on Day-7 return rate for this user profile?
How should the AI coaching agent tier its model usage (e.g., GPT-4o-mini for routine interactions, GPT-4o for high-stakes personalization moments) to stay cost-viable at scale?
What is the minimum onboarding data collection required to generate a genuinely personalized first workout without creating form fatigue that drives abandonment?
How does TransformFit credibly differentiate from Fitbod's adaptive strength engine given identical budget and solo-founder constraints — what is the defensible wedge?
